subject: Astronomy & Space
title: Halley's Comet
description: A fast breakdown of Halley's Comet for builders.

hook.text: Halley's Comet visits Earth every 75 years. Humans have recorded it for over 2,000 years.

buildup.text: Edmund Halley used Newton's gravity to predict its return in 1758. He was right but died before seeing it.

discovery.text: The tail forms when solar heat vaporizes surface ice. The solar wind pushes the gas away from the Sun.

twist.text: The Bayeux Tapestry shows Halley's Comet in 1066. Harold of England saw it before losing his throne.

climax.text: It last appeared in 1986. The next visit is 2061. Some alive today will see it twice.

punchline.text: One comet, millennia of wonder. Next visit: 2061.

quiz.question: How did Edmund Halley predict the comet's return?
options: He used Newton's laws of gravity to calculate its orbit
He found ancient records showing a regular pattern
He observed the comet moving in real time and projected its path
